presentation.ask_text: The agent proposes a package whose name is one character away from the approved dependency.
presentation.agent_note: It says this package is a drop-in replacement for requests.

artifacts.title: Install proposal
artifacts.content:
pip install reqeusts==2.32.0
Approved lockfile entry: requests==2.32.0
Registry policy: reqeusts is not approved

decision.reasoning_text: The proposed library name differs by transposed letters from the dependency our project normally uses, and its origin has not been established.

critic_proof.followup: The learner identifies the transposed-letter package-name mismatch. They do not explicitly identify that the package is absent from the approved lockfile.

receipts.claim: The proposed name differs from the approved dependency and the supplied policy marks it unapproved.
receipts.evidence: reqeusts versus requests; registry policy says not approved.

grade.explanation: A plausible near-miss is a supply-chain warning. Reject it and use the exact locked dependency after verifying provenance.
grade.socratic_followup: The learner identifies the transposed-letter package-name mismatch. They do not explicitly identify that the package is absent from the approved lockfile.
